name: Comics
description: Superhero comics, manga, strips, and the comic book industry

questions.q: Who is Superman's main love interest, a reporter at the Daily Planet?

source.notes: Created by Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster. First appeared in Action Comics #1 (1938).

questions.q: What is Supergirl's relationship to Superman?

source.notes: Real name Kara Zor-El. First appeared in Action Comics #252 (1959).

questions.q: What material coats Wolverine's skeleton and claws?

source.notes: Grafted via the Weapon X program. Virtually indestructible Marvel-universe alloy.

questions.q: In X-Men, who is the cosmic entity that possesses Jean Grey?

source.notes: Massive cosmic power grants Jean godlike abilities. Leads to the 'Dark Phoenix Saga.'

questions.q: Which 1938 comic book introduced Superman, kickstarting the superhero genre?

source.notes: Superman's debut. A copy sold for $6M in 2024, making it one of the most valuable comics.

questions.q: What comic series introduced Batman in 1939?

source.notes: Detective Comics #27. The 'DC' in DC Comics comes from this title.

questions.q: Which British comic, launched in 1938, is still published today?

source.notes: Home to Dennis the Menace, Minnie the Minx, and other classic British comic characters.

questions.q: What is Peter Parker's day job in the comics?

source.notes: Photographer at the Daily Bugle. J. Jonah Jameson is his boss.

questions.q: How many Robins have served as Batman's sidekick in the main DC continuity?

source.notes: Dick Grayson, Jason Todd, Tim Drake, Stephanie Brown, Damian Wayne. (Sometimes cited as 4-5 depending on who counts.)

questions.q: Which company publishes Spider-Man and X-Men comics?

source.notes: Marvel (originally Atlas, Timely). DC publishes Batman, Superman, Wonder Woman, etc.

questions.q: Eric Wimp transforms into which banana-fueled British comic superhero?

source.notes: British parody of American superheroes. Debuted in Nutty comic, 1980.

questions.q: Which Japanese comic style is known for black-and-white panels read right-to-left?

source.notes: Japanese word for 'comics' or 'cartoons.' 'Shojo' and 'doujinshi' are subcategories.

questions.q: What is the name of the human form of the character known as the Hulk?

source.notes: Dr. Robert Bruce Banner, nuclear physicist. Created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby (1962).

questions.q: Who is Batman's butler?

source.notes: Alfred is Bruce Wayne's father figure and confidant. First appeared in Batman #16 (1943).

questions.q: Which X-Men character is a blue, teleporting mutant?

source.notes: Kurt Wagner. Teleports via a BAMF! sound. Mother is Mystique, father is the demon Azazel.

questions.q: Galactus is famous in Marvel Comics for what?

source.notes: Cosmic being who consumes planets to survive. Silver Surfer is his herald.

questions.q: Sentinels are giant robots used by whom to hunt mutants?

source.notes: Created by Bolivar Trask in X-Men comics. Featured in 'Days of Future Past' storyline.

questions.q: Archie Andrews, Betty, Veronica, and Jughead live in which fictional town?

source.notes: First appeared 1941. Published by Archie Comics. Inspired CW's 'Riverdale' TV series.

questions.q: What was the creative partnership between artist Jack Kirby and writer Stan Lee known for?

source.notes: Co-created Fantastic Four, X-Men, Hulk, Thor, Iron Man, Avengers in the 1960s.

questions.q: Which Marvel character is a master archer and often teamed with the Avengers?

source.notes: Clint Barton. Former circus performer. First appeared Tales of Suspense #57 (1964).
